name: Chocolate Cherry Valentine Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour three heart-shaped baking pans or three 9-inch round baking pans.
2. In a small bowl, beat egg yolks until foamy. Gradually add 3/4 cup sugar, beating until stiff peaks form. Set aside.
3. In a large bowl, stir together flour, remaining 1 cup sugar, cocoa, baking soda, and salt. Add oil, buttermilk, and egg yolks; beat until smooth. Gently fold in the beaten egg yolks.
4. Pour about 2/3 cup batter into each prepared pan. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes or until the cake springs back when lightly touched in the center. Cool on wire racks for 5 minutes. Remove from pans and cool completely.
5. Prepare Cream Filling: Place one layer on a serving plate. Pipe or spoon a 1/2-inch border around the outer edge. Spread half of the cherry pie filling in the center. Top with the second cake layer. Spread with half of the remaining cream filling. Top with the third layer. Spoon remaining pie filling on top, within 1 inch of the edge.
6. Prepare Chocolate Whipped Cream and frost the sides of the cake. Pipe the top edge with the remaining cream filling. Refrigerate until serving time. Cover; store leftovers in the refrigerator. Makes 8 to 10 servings.
To make sour milk: Mix 1 tablespoon white vinegar with 1 cup milk.
If only two pans are available, reserve the remaining batter in the refrigerator for the third layer while the first two layers bake.
